topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax deductions and credits for individuals and property.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow all individual taxpayers to deduct from gross income state, local, and foreign real property taxes
whether or not such taxpayers itemize their tax deductions.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2013 the tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums.
Document 3: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through December 31, 2018, the tax credit for qualified fuel cell property.

topic.name: Healthcare Accessibility Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to healthcare services and support for individuals with specific health needs.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to conduct pilot projects under title XVIII (Medicare)
providing incentives to home health agencies to utilize home monitoring and communications technology.
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
to give states the option to disregard certain income in providing continued Medicaid coverage for certain individuals with extremely high annual lifelong orphan drug costs.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
to eliminate the in-the-home restriction for Medicare coverage of mobility devices for individuals with expected long-term needs.

topic.name: Higher Education Funding and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing financial support and regulatory standards for higher education institutions and students.

Document 1: Year-Round Pell Grant Restoration Act
require the Department of Education to award an additional Pell Grant
Document 2: amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act
allocate and award funds to outlying areas under the State Fiscal Stabilization Fund program
Document 3: Online Education Verification Act
require recognized accrediting agencies to evaluate the quality of distance education programs

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: Legislation related to the amendment of the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States for various goods and materials.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on 1,1,2-2-Tetrafluoroethene, oxidized, polymerized.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on fine animal hair of Kashmir (cashmere) goats, processed beyond the degreased or carbonized condition.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to temporarily suspend the duty on certain programmable slow cookers.
temporarily suspend the duty

topic.name: Veterans' Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving health care access and compensation for veterans, particularly regarding mental health services and related costs.

Document 1: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for veterans' disability compensation
Compensation owed for mental health based on activities in theater
Document 2: Amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act
Permits VA to recover charges for non-service-connected disability care
Document 3: Makes veterans eligible for mental health care at non-VA facilities
Eligibility regardless of enrollment date or appointment attempts

topic.name: Offshore Energy Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to oil and gas leasing and pipeline projects in the Outer Continental Shelf and Alaska.

Document 1: Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Improvement Act of 2011
amends the Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Act of 2004
Document 2: allows leasing of areas in the Outer Continental Shelf
effective upon the termination of such prohibition
Document 3: Offshore Energy and Jobs Act of 2015
directs the Department of the Interior to make available for leasing

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the U.S. immigration system, including visa programs, refugee admissions, and provisions for foreign students.

Document 1: Visa Waiver Program Enhanced Security and Reform Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act regarding the visa waiver program
authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to designate any country as a program country
Document 2: This bill prohibits the obligation of federal funds for the U.S. refugee admission of any alien
whose country of nationality is Syria
Document 3: Strengthening America's Public Schools Through Promoting Foreign Investment Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act
to permit a lawful alien who enters the United States on a nonimmigrant student visa to attend a U.S. public secondary school

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislation related to the assessment and accountability of foreign electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report on research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program.

topic.name: Wilderness Area Designation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation and management of wilderness areas as part of the National Wilderness Preservation System in various states.

Document 1: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness
Provides for the inclusion of specified potential wilderness land within the Park in such Wilderness.
Document 2: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado managed by the Bureau of Land Management
Designates specified lands in Colorado managed by the BLM or the U.S. Forest Service as wilderness.
Document 3: Designates the following areas in California as wilderness areas and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System
certain land in Sequoia-Kings Canyon National Park, which shall be known as the John Krebs Wilderness

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the welfare, protection, and rights of children and vulnerable populations in the context of foster care and elder justice.

Document 1: require state child protection standards to ensure that the immigration status alone of a parent, legal guardian, or relative shall not disqualify the parent, legal guardian, or relative from being a placement for a child
Includes as a compelling reason for a state not to file for termination of parental rights in foster care cases the removal of an otherwise fit and willing parent from the United States
Document 2: establish an Elder Justice program under title XX
provides funding to state and local adult protective services services offices that investigate reports of elder abuse, neglect, and exploitation
Document 3: ensure that children in foster care placements in public or private agencies receive quality services that protect their safety and health
support services to assist with care of the child

topic.name: Autism Spectrum Disorders and Mental Health
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at improving research, services, and support for individuals with autism spectrum disorders and mental health needs of older adults.

Document 1: establish the National Autism Spectrum Disorders Initiative
improve the lives of persons with autism spectrum disorders through research focused on prevention, treatment, services, and cures
Document 2: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to public and private nonprofit entities
integrating mental health services for older adults into primary care settings
Document 3: expand activities to include coordinating efforts of all federal agencies
require the Committee to establish specified subcommittees

topic.name: Pharmaceutical Regulation and Fees
topic.description: This topic encompasses the regulatory framework and fee structures associated with the approval and monitoring of pharmaceutical products, including generic drugs and biosimilars.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS), beginning FY2013, to assess and collect the following fees related to generic drugs
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress on the progress of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in achieving specified safety, access, and transparency goals with respect to generic drugs.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish requirements to facilitate the tracing of drug products through the pharmaceutical supply distribution chain.

Requires a manufacturer, wholesale distributor, dispenser, repackager, in the event of a recall or for the purpose of investigating a suspect prescription drug product or an illegitimate prescription drug product, to provide within two business days the applicable transaction documentation upon request by the Secretary or other appropriate federal or state official.

topic.name: Water Rights and Reclamation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at authorizing, planning, and funding water rights settlements and reclamation projects across various regions.

Document 1: Bill Williams River Water Rights Settlement Act of 2014
Confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act
Authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility
Document 3: Avra/Black Wash Reclamation and Riparian Restoration Project
Enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Fraud Prevention
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of mortgage lending practices and the prevention of fraud within financial institutions.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to include within the definition of 'financial institution' a mortgage lending business
Extends the prohibition against making false statements in a mortgage application to employees and agents of a mortgage lending business.
Document 2: Amends the S.A.F.E. Mortgage Licensing Act of 2008 to exempt from certain licensing and registration requirements
Prohibits, in determining whether a residential mortgage loan is a qualified mortgage, from applying to loans originated by such a person certain guidelines and regulations.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the protection and restoration of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
establish the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to coordinate federal actions
Document 2: direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems
authorize the Secretary to conduct activities to conserve coral reef ecosystems
Document 3: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to expand the coral reef conservation program
NOAA must establish the Center of Excellence in Coral Reef Ecosystem Science Cooperative Institute

topic.name: Veterans in Agriculture
topic.description: Legislation aimed at supporting military veterans in agricultural pursuits through various programs and funding opportunities.

Document 1: Amends the Agricultural Risk Protection Act regarding the value-added agricultural product market development grants program to include farmers and ranchers who are veterans among priority recipients.

Establishes the position of Military Veterans Agricultural Liaison to provide returning veterans with information about beginning farmer training and agricultural vocational and rehabilitation programs.
Document 2: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to provide agricultural producers who produce bioenergy with commodity storage capacity loans.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on promoting renewable and alternative fuels in transportation and electricity generation.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the Renewable Fuel Program
define 'domestic alternative fuel' as ethanol that is produced from domestic fossil fuels
Document 2: amends the Clean Air Act to apply limitations on Reid vapor pressure
limitations on Reid vapor pressure are placed on gasoline blended with more than 10% ethanol
Document 3: Amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978
require each electric utility to obtain a percentage of electric energy from clean energy

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative efforts aimed at enhancing the protection of personally identifiable information and ensuring accountability in the event of data breaches.

Document 1: the General Services Administration to procure an antivirus product suitable for federal agencies
provide to any individual directly affected a one-year license of such product
Document 2: expand requirements for reports to Congress on acquisition or disclosure of unsecured protected health information
the identity of the covered entity involved in each breach
Document 3: impose a fine and/or prison term of up to five years for intentionally or willfully concealing a security breach
makes it unlawful for a service provider to knowingly or intentionally redirect web searches or otherwise monitor, manipulate, aggregate, and market data

topic.name: Tax Fraud and Identity Theft Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the prevention and investigation of tax fraud and identity theft through IRS reforms and information sharing.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code relating to laundering of monetary instruments to expand the concept of monetary proceeds to include gross receipts.
Directs the U.S. Sentencing Commission to amend its sentencing guidelines to incorporate the amendment made by this Act.
Document 2: Directs the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to provide a taxpayer who is selected for an audit sufficient information regarding the reason the taxpayer's return was selected for an audit.
Prohibits the IRS from conducting any audit, investigation, or examination of a taxpayer that is primarily for research purposes.
Document 3: Authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to disclose tax return information to federal and state law enforcement agencies to assist in the investigation of the misuse of the identity of another person.
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to implement an identity theft tax fraud prevention program.

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation and oversight of financial institutions and their activities.

Document 1: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956
prohibits financial holding companies and national banks from engaging in real estate brokerage or management activities.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act
requires the FDIC to resolve depository institution holding companies of failed or failing insured depository institutions.
Document 3: Prohibits Members of Congress and senior staff from trading registered securities
prohibits bonuses for executives at government-sponsored enterprises during conservatorship.

topic.name: Wildlife and Animal Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at the protection and regulation of wildlife and animals, including hunting, habitat conservation, and the prevention of animal cruelty.

Document 1: Amends the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 to exempt from sequestration the following budget accounts: Federal Aid in Wildlife Restoration, Sport Fish Restoration, and Wildlife Restoration.
Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 to direct the Secretary of the Interior to issue a permit for the importation of any polar bear part.
Document 2: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973, with respect to critical habitat designations, to exclude an area from critical habitat upon a determination that the benefits of exclusion outweigh the benefits of including the area.

Requires the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Commerce... to publish and make available for public comment a draft quantitative analysis that examines the economic effects of the proposed designation.
Document 3: This bill amends the Horse Protection Act to establish a new system for inspecting horses for soring, revise penalties for violations of the Act.
The bill prohibits a person in any horse show, exhibition, sale, or auction from causing or directing a horse to become sore.

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures and amendments related to postal services, IRS employee conduct, and Medicare practices.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 489 Army Drive in Barrigada, Guam, as the 'John Pangelinan Gerber Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Service Restructuring and Reform Act of 1998 to expand existing grounds for termination of the employment of an Internal Revenue Service employee.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to increase from a three-year to a five-year period the length of an agreement with an independence at home medical practice.

topic.name: Rail Transportation Safety and Development
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing rail safety and promoting development of passenger rail systems in the United States.

Document 1: promote intercity and urban passenger rail operations
authorize the Secretary to provide direct loans and loan guarantees for qualified rail projects
Document 2: authorize the Department of Transportation (DOT) to extend the deadline for implementation of positive train control systems
study the effectiveness of positive train control and related technologies on reducing collisions at highway-rail grade crossings
Document 3: prohibit the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) from authorizing any person to operate an unmanned aircraft system (drone) as a weapon

topic.name: Nuclear Energy and STEM Education
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to nuclear energy management and the promotion of STEM education to support technological advancements.

Document 1: Prohibit the Department of Energy from planning or undertaking sales, trades, barters, or transfers of any metal that has been used or stored in a radiological area
Requires storage or disposal of such metal at a site specifically licensed for radioactive waste.
Document 2: Enhance the U.S. capability in fusion engineering science to ensure full U.S. benefit from the International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor project
Develop a comprehensive plan identifying the full range of research and development needed to achieve practical fusion energy.
Document 3: Direct the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to local educational agencies to improve science, technology, engineering, and mathematics education
Requires grantees to collaborate with institutions of higher education and local employers in STEM fields.

topic.name: Retirement and Pension Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, security, and advice related to retirement plans and pension funds.

Document 1: amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA)
defines 'investment advice' as a recommendation that relates to...
Document 2: Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA)
requires assets of single-employer pension plans to be held in trust by joint boards of trustees.
Document 3: directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make available a new federal income tax Form 1040S
allows the form to be used even if income includes...

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and funding for small businesses through various programs and initiatives.

Document 1: Establishes a three-year small business intermediary lending pilot program
make direct loans to eligible nonprofit lending intermediaries
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act to increase the amount that each federal agency must spend on SBIR programs
increases the amounts which such agency may award in the first and second phases of STTR programs
Document 3: amends the Small Business Act with respect to the authority of the Small Business Administration
provide grants, financial assistance, loans, export assistance, and subcontracting opportunities

topic.name: Tribal Governance and Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the governance, self-determination, and legal recognition of Indian tribes in the United States.

Document 1: prohibits any Indian tribe or a tribal organization from cultivating, manufacturing, or distributing marijuana on Indian lands
No funds authorized or appropriated by federal law shall be made available to an Indian tribe or a tribal organization determined to have violated this Act
Document 2: Amends the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act to revise tribal self-governance requirements
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to carry out the Tribal Self-Governance Program
Document 3: provides for the consideration of a petition for federal recognition as an Indian tribe
expresses the sense of the Congress that review of such petition should not unnecessarily delay review of petitions awaiting active consideration

topic.name: Social Security Reform
topic.description: Legislative amendments aimed at modifying the Social Security Act to address benefits and solvency issues.

Document 1: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to provide for progressive indexing of old age insurance benefits.
Acceleration of the increase in retirement age to 67.
Document 2: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to require reduced to zero the total of an individual's monthly disability insurance benefits.
Allows only unemployment compensation in such a situation.

topic.name: Executive Branch Regulation Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing oversight and accountability of executive branch agencies in their rulemaking and budgetary processes.

Document 1: Rewrites provisions regarding congressional review of agency rulemaking
Requires congressional approval of major rules before they may take effect.
Document 2: Requires all executive branch agencies to include a separate category for administrative expenses
Directs each agency to submit a budget to OMB for administrative expenses that reflects a reduction.
Document 3: Revises rulemaking requirements under the Unfunded Mandates Reform Act
Requires federal agencies to prepare and publish regulatory impact analysis prior to promulgating any proposed or final rule.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to U.S. foreign policy, particularly concerning sanctions and restrictions on nations such as Iran and Syria.

Document 1: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the prohibition against participation in the visa waiver program
prohibition against participation in the visa waiver program of an alien who is a national of Iraq or Syria
Document 2: prohibits the Department of the Treasury from issuing a license
transactions that would involve or benefit the government of Iran
Document 3: prohibits any agreement for cooperation to enter into force
government of the UAE has taken actions to prohibit the transfer of goods, services, or technology to the government of Iran

topic.name: Homelessness and Education Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving educational access and housing assistance for homeless individuals, particularly children and families.

Document 1: Homeless Education Improvement Act of 2007 - Amends the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act to require each state and local educational agency (LEA) to ensure that each homeless child and each homeless youth has access to the same free, appropriate public education.
Prohibits segregation of such children and youths into separate schools or programs within schools.
Document 2: Moving to Work Charter Program Act of 2007 - Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to enter into charter contracts with public housing agencies administering the public housing program.
Shall ensure that at least 75% of the families assisted are very low-income families.
Document 3: Homeless Children and Youth Act of 2014 - Amends the McKinney-Vento Homeless Assistance Act to redefine 'homeless,' 'homeless individual,' or 'homeless person.'

Requires the information provided to the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) from a collaborative applicant about project sponsors in a community-wide homeless management information system (HMIS) to be made publicly available.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating campaign expenditures and funding sources for elections in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to limit expenditures by candidates for the House of Representatives to $500,000 during the election cycle.
Excludes from such limits expenditures for legal services.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to set forth specified expenditure limitations for House of Representatives general elections.
Establishes in the Treasury the Grassroots Good Citizenship Fund (Fund), which shall provide public funding for House candidates' expenditures.
Document 3: Prohibits the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) from making lobbying expenditures or political contributions.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at protecting consumer rights and interests in different contexts, including finance and environmental regulations.

Document 1: amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act
authorizes Alaska Natives to import marine mammal products
Document 2: repeals the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
revives provisions of law amended by such Act
Document 3: amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act
requires consumer reporting agency to include documentation provided by the consumer

topic.name: Broadcast and Communication Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative acts related to the regulation of broadcasting and communication services in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to allow any cable system or other multichannel video programming distributor to retransmit the signal of a broadcasting station only with the express authority of that station.
Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to commence a rulemaking proceeding to revise regulations governing the exercise by broadcast stations of the right to grant such consent.
Document 2: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to initiate a rulemaking to rechannelize the public safety spectrum to accommodate commercially available broadband applications.
Document 3: Requires the FCC to modify its rules to eliminate third-adjacent minimum distance separation requirements between specified stations.
Requires the FCC, when licensing FM translator stations, to ensure that licenses are available to both FM translator stations and low-power FM stations.

topic.name: Military Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to benefits and support for military service members and their families, including survivor benefits, death gratuities, and disability considerations.

Document 1: extends special survivor indemnity allowance for a surviving or former spouse of a deceased member of the Armed Forces
whose annuity under the survivor benefit program is offset by the amount of dependency and indemnity compensation
Document 2: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to transfer funds to the Secretary of Defense for payment of death gratuities
payment of authorized funeral and burial expenses for deceased military service members
Document 3: requires that a member of the reserves who is retired or placed on the temporary disability retired list be credited with the number of years of service for retired pay

topic.name: Consumer Product Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety standards and disclosure requirements for consumer products, particularly those intended for children and hazardous materials like lead.

Document 1: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Act to require comprehensive testing for articles intended for children under five.
Requires the CPSC to establish a database for information regarding factories or facilities where recalled products were manufactured.
Document 2: Amends the Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act to govern the disclosure of lead-based paint hazards.
Imposes civil liability on violators who fail to disclose lead-based paint hazards.

topic.name: Workforce Development and Employment Rights
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing workforce training, employment rights, and economic mobility for workers in various sectors.

Document 1: establish and carry out a health professions training demonstration project
train certain unemployed workers from the manufacturing or service sector for employment as health care professionals
Document 2: grants an employee the right to request that his or her employer change the terms and conditions of employment
requires the employer to engage in a timely, good faith interactive process with the employee
Document 3: require every employer to pay to each of his or her eligible Puerto Rican employees minimum wages of $5.00 an hour
treat certain employers who make qualified wage subsidy payments to eligible Puerto Rico employees as having paid payroll taxes

topic.name: Immigration Detention Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures and policies related to the detention and treatment of individuals, particularly non-U.S. nationals, within the context of U.S. immigration enforcement and military custody.

Document 1: This bill prohibits U.S. agency funds from being used until the earlier of the enactment date of an Act authorizing FY2017 appropriations for military activities of the Department of Defense (DOD) or January 21, 2017, for the transfer or release to or within the United States, its territories, or possessions or to any foreign country or entity of an individual detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
Individual detained at Guantanamo means an individual located at Guantanamo as of October 1, 2009, who: (1) is not a U.S. national or a member of the Armed Forces.
Document 2: Makes an alien who is detained at the Department of Defense (DOD) detention facility at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, permanently ineligible for: (1) U.S. admission for any purpose, including refugee admission.
Document 3: Sets forth apprehension procedures for immigration enforcement-related activities engaged in by the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) and cooperating entities.

topic.name: School Health and Literacy Initiatives
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving health services and literacy resources in under-resourced schools.

Document 1: Nurses for Under-Resourced Schools Everywhere Act
student-to-school nurse ratio in each of their public elementary and secondary schools is 750 or more
Document 2: Student-to-School Nurse Ratio Improvement Act of 2011
priority to LEAs that serve a high number or percentage of impoverished students
Document 3: Strengthening Kids' Interest in Learning and Libraries Act
Improving Literacy through School Libraries grant program

topic.name: Election and Redistricting Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving the fairness and integrity of electoral processes and redistricting in the United States.

Document 1: Requires any congressional redistricting conducted by a state to be conducted in accordance with a redistricting plan developed by a nonpartisan agency
Requires the state legislature to approve or reject the plan within three days after receiving the Commission's report.
Document 2: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to revise requirements for the treatment of provisional ballots in federal elections
Prohibits any election recount until all provisional votes have been counted.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Air Force to carry out a demonstration program to assess the feasibility and advisability of permitting individuals with auditory impairments access as officers of the Air Force
Authorizes between 15 and 20 individuals who are deaf or have a range of other auditory impairments to participate in such program.

topic.name: Federal Legislation on Crime and Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal laws related to the prevention of cruelty, firearm possession regulations, and public safety measures.

Document 1: Prevention of Equine Cruelty Act of 2008

impose a fine and/or prison term of up to three years for possessing, shipping, transporting, purchasing, selling, delivering, or receiving any horse with the intent that it be slaughtered for human consumption.
Document 2: Bars the promulgation or enforcement of any regulation that prohibits an individual from possessing a firearm in any unit of the National Park System
the possession of the firearm is in compliance with the law of the state in which the unit is located.
Document 3: prohibit any person from carrying a firearm at a place which the person knows is a restricted firearms zone
Defines 'restricted firearms zone' to mean within 1,000 feet of any structure in which a senior federal official is holding an official event.

topic.name: Aviation Regulations and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and initiatives related to aviation safety, environmental impact, and security measures in the aviation industry.

Document 1: establish a program to reduce or remove lead from the emissions of piston engine aircraft.
Document 2: require each air carrier that submits an airline service quality performance report to also submit a monthly report on each diverted flight and canceled flight of the air carrier.
Document 3: prescribe employment standards for air carrier personnel and airport security personnel.
develop partnerships with state and local law enforcement agencies to improve coordination of behavior detection activities.

topic.name: Food Labeling Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at standardizing and improving nutritional and safety labeling on food products.

Document 1: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to revise the nutritional information that restaurants and retail food establishments must disclose
Nutritional information may be provided solely by a remote-access menu
Document 2: establish requirements that address food waste due to confusion over expiration dates
standardize quality date and safety date food labels
Document 3: Directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs to promulgate a final regulation revising federal regulations applicable to the declaration of the trans fat content on a food label

topic.name: Native American and Hawaiian Affairs
topic.description: Legislation focused on the recognition, health, and tourism initiatives for Native American and Native Hawaiian communities.

Document 1: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages of Haines, Ketchikan, Petersburg, Tenakee, and Wrangell, Alaska, to organize as Urban Corporations
to receive certain settlement lands and other compensation pursuant to this Act.
Document 2: Reauthorizes for FY2011-FY2016 and revises the Native Hawaiian Health Care Improvement Act
supports community-based initiatives that reflect holistic approaches to health.
Document 3: requires the Department of Commerce, the Department of the Interior, and federal agencies with recreational travel or tourism functions to update their management plans and tourism initiatives to include Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and Native Hawaiian organizations
assist in interpreting the connections between Native Americans and the national identity of the United States.

topic.name: Legal Protections and Jurisdiction
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at protecting the rights of individuals, particularly servicemembers, and addressing jurisdictional issues within federal courts.

Document 1: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to allow the use of arbitration to resolve a controversy that arises under a contract with a servicemember
Allows a representative of members of an aggrieved class to bring a private civil action for a violation of any rights or protections under such Act.
Document 2: Amends the federal judicial code with respect to the jurisdiction of federal courts over civil actions pending in, or on appeal from, the U.S. Court of Federal Claims
Requires the court presiding over the action that was filed later to stay the later action until the action that was filed first is no longer pending.
Document 3: Authorizes a designated state official to file with the head of a federal agency proposing a rule, a legal brief challenging the constitutionality of the rule under the Tenth Amendment
Directs the relevant U.S. Court of Appeals to grant expedited review of a decision by a district court in such a case.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Innovations
topic.description: Legislative efforts to expand the powers and services of the U.S. Postal Service, including the provision of nonpostal services and innovative revenue strategies.

Document 1: Expands the enumerated powers of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to include the authority to provide nonpostal services.
Removes existing conditions that limit nonpostal services to certain services offered as of January 1, 2006.
Document 2: Authorizes the United States Postal Service (USPS) to provide nonpostal services (e.g., check-cashing services, warehousing, public internet access services).
Requires the USPS Board of Governors to ensure that the rates and fees charged by USPS are at least equal to the total costs for providing postal services.
Document 3: Establishes in USPS the position of the Chief Innovation Officer who shall have proven expertise and success in the areas of innovation, research and development, and marketing.
Requires the Postmaster General to submit to Congress a comprehensive strategy for maximizing revenues through innovative postal and nonpostal products and services.

topic.name: Community Policing and Crime Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing law enforcement capabilities and preventing crime through community engagement and targeted programs.

Document 1: hire former honorably-discharged members of the Armed Forces to serve as career law enforcement officers
community-oriented policing, particularly in communities that are adversely affected by a recent military base closing.
Document 2: waive matching fund requirements applicable to the grant program for the purchase of armor vests for law enforcement officers
extend such program through FY2012.
Document 3: expand prohibitions against criminal street gangs
impose criminal penalties on individuals who knowingly commit, or conspire, threaten, or attempt to commit, a gang crime in furtherance of a criminal street gang.

topic.name: Child Abortion Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on parental involvement and restrictions regarding minors seeking abortions across state lines.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
deems such transporting to be a de facto abridgment of the right of a parent
Document 2: require an abortion provider to inform the woman of the probable age of the child
creates an exception to save the life of the mother
Document 3: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
imposes a fine and/or prison term on a physician who violates parental notification requirements

topic.name: Fire Safety and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on fire safety education, management, and emergency response funding.

Document 1: Establishes the Federal Land Assistance, Management, and Enhancement Fund (Flame Fund) for wildland fire suppression activities.
Requires the Secretaries to report annually on the use of the funds from the Flame Fund.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to establish a program to award grants for fire safety education programs.
Requires institutions to use grant funds to improve fire safety education and awareness among students.

topic.name: Military Sexual Assault Response
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the prevention and response to sexual assault within the military and Department of Defense.

Document 1: Establishes within the Department of Defense a Director of the Sexual Assault Prevention and Response Office
Requires the assignment within each military department of at least one full-time Sexual Assault Response Coordinator and one full-time Sexual Assault Victim Advocate
Document 2: Amends the Ike Skelton National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2011 to require the Director of the Sexual Assault Prevention and Response Office to be appointed from general or flag officers
Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish a professional training and certification program for such coordinators and advocates

topic.name: Legislative Changes and Autonomy
topic.description: This topic encompasses recent amendments and acts that affect legislative authority and the regulation of various activities at both federal and local levels.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to authorize the Attorney General (AG) to commence an in rem action against a domain name used by an Internet site that is 'dedicated to infringing activities.'

Defines an Internet site that is 'dedicated to infringing activities' as a site that is: (1) subject to civil forfeiture; (2) designed primarily to offer goods or services in violation of federal copyright law; or (3) selling or promoting counterfeit goods or services.
Document 2: Amends the Hatch Act to eliminate the prohibition against state and local employees seeking partisan elective office.
Document 3: Amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act to eliminate congressional review of newly-passed District laws.

topic.name: Trade and Economic Policy
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures related to trade regulations, currency valuation, and domestic production requirements in the context of economic policy.

Document 1: Currency Reform for Fair Trade Act of 2009
determine whether the exchange rate of the currency of an exporting country is fundamentally and actionably undervalued or overvalued
Document 2: no antidumping duty deposit shall be required upon entry
no antidumping duty be assessed on it
Document 3: Buy America for Drinking Water Extension Act
require projects funded by the drinking water state revolving fund to only use iron and steel produced in the United States

topic.name: International Development and Health
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving health, sanitation, and education in developing countries through international assistance programs.

Document 1: Directs the Comptroller General (GAO) to report to Congress on the status of post-earthquake recovery and development efforts in Haiti
efforts to prevent the spread of cholera and treat persons infected with the disease.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to direct the Administrator of USAID to establish the Office of Water
place primary emphasis on providing safe drinking water, sanitation, and hygiene.
Document 3: Directs the Administrator for USAID to establish and administer a health and education grant program
support activities by nongovernmental organizations and other service providers, including advocacy groups, focused on autism in developing countries.

topic.name: Health and Social Welfare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on health care access, privacy, and support for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: prohibit wellness programs from sharing with an employer information related to an individual's use of contraceptives.
Document 2: adjust to permanent resident status a qualifying Liberian national
continuously present in the United States between January 1, 2013, through the date of status adjustment application.
Document 3: reauthorize lifespan respite care programs
services for family caregivers of children and adults with special needs

topic.name: Tax-Exempt Financing Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to tax-exempt bonds and financing provisions for various sectors including municipal bonds, mortgage bonds, and agricultural financing.

Document 1: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to the small issuer exemption from interest expense allocation rules for financial institutions
increase from $10 to $30 million the annual limit on small issuers of tax-exempt municipal bonds
Document 2: allow proceeds of qualified mortgage bonds to be used to refinance certain residential subprime loans
exempt tax-exempt interest on qualified mortgage or veterans' mortgage bonds issued before 2011 from the alternative minimum tax
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to private activity bond financing for first-time farmers
increase from $450,000 to $520,000 the amount of bond proceeds that may be used by a first-time farmer to acquire land for farming purposes

topic.name: Civil Rights and Anti-Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at prohibiting discrimination based on race, color, national origin, sexual orientation, and gender identity in housing, employment, and federally assisted programs.

Document 1: discrimination based on disparate impact under federally assisted programs
bring a civil action in federal or state court to enforce such person's rights
Document 2: prohibited discrimination in housing sales and rentals
adds to the list of prohibited actual or perceived bases sexual orientation, gender identity
Document 3: prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity
provides for enforcement, including giving the EEOC... the same enforcement powers as they have under specified provisions of the Civil Rights Act of 1964

topic.name: Commemorative Commissions and Federal Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses the establishment and oversight of commemorative commissions aimed at honoring significant historical events and figures, along with the evaluation of federal programs.

Document 1: amends the Act that authorized the Adams Memorial Foundation to establish a commemorative work
establish the Adams Memorial Commission to establish a permanent memorial
Document 2: establish a Civil War Sesquicentennial Commemoration Commission
to plan, develop, and carry out programs and activities appropriate to commemorate the sesquicentennial of the Civil War
Document 3: establishes the Federal Program Sunset Commission
to evaluate the efficiency and public need for each federal program

topic.name: Tax Incentives and Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to tax incentives and reforms in various sectors, particularly focusing on biodiesel and other tax-related amendments.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the income and excise tax credits for biodiesel
extend the biodiesel income and excise tax credits through December 31, 2018.
Document 2: Repeals the provision of the Internal Revenue Code
Rescinds $39 billion of appropriated but unobligated discretionary funds.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2015 the increase in the amount of distilled spirits tax

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Relations and Military Support
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses U.S. legislative measures aimed at enhancing educational partnerships and military support in relation to Vietnam and Taiwan.

Document 1: Establishes the Vietnam Education Foundation within the Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs of the Department of State.
Authorizes the Secretary of State... to award grants to institutions of higher education for a partnership with the government of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam.
Document 2: directs the President to develop a strategy to obtain observer status for Taiwan in the International Criminal Police Organization (INTERPOL)
Document 3: a critical element to maintaining peace and stability in Asia... is ensuring a militarily strong Taiwan
the United States has a statutory obligation under the Taiwan Relations Act to help Taiwan maintain sufficient self-defense capabilities.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing various public health challenges and diseases in the United States.

Document 1: makes Department of Defense (DOD) funds available for the deployment of insect repellant and other appropriate measures for members of the Armed Forces and DOD civilian personnel stationed in or deployed to areas affected or that may soon be affected by the Zika virus
DOD shall submit a strategy to counter Zika should it become a long-term issue
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to report to Congress on the epidemiology of, impact of, and appropriate funding required to address neglected diseases of poverty
address the threat of such diseases
Document 3: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS), acting through the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), to establish and maintain an undiagnosed diseases registry
facilitate the early recognition, prevention, treatment, cure, and control of such diseases

topic.name: Sales Tax and Digital Goods Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the taxation of sales, particularly focusing on remote sales and digital goods, while addressing issues of fairness and privacy in electronic transactions.

Document 1: Grants the consent of Congress to the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Agreement
to require remote sellers to collect and remit the sales and use taxes
Document 2: Prohibits a state or local jurisdiction from imposing multiple or discriminatory taxes on digital goods
expresses the sense of Congress that each state shall take reasonable steps to prevent multiple taxation

topic.name: Workplace Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving safety standards and staffing regulations in various workplace environments, including healthcare and mining.

Document 1: Nurse Staffing Standards for Patient Safety and Quality Care Act of 2007 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to require hospitals to implement staffing plans that meet specified ratios for direct care registered nurse-to-patient staffing levels for each unit
Requires hospitals to provide the Secretary with their staffing plan and annual updates.
Document 2: Robert C. Byrd Mine and Workplace Safety and Health Act of 2010 - Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the Secretary of Labor to conduct health and safety related accident investigations in coal or other mines
Requires an independent accident investigation by an independent panel appointed by the Secretary of Health and Human Services for any accident involving three or more deaths.

topic.name: Emergency Unemployment Compensation
topic.description: Legislation and policies related to unemployment benefits and their adjustments for individuals and states.

Document 1: Subjects an individual's emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) benefit payment to a gradual reduction
the amount of EUC payable to any individual reduced by 5%
Document 2: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to revise the formula for determining if a state is in an extended benefit period
Modifies the threshold rates of unemployment that must be met by a state in order to establish eligibility for Tier-4 emergency unemployment compensation (EUC)

topic.name: Regulation of Sports and Gambling
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating various forms of sports, gambling, and related industries.

Document 1: impose an Internet gambling license fee on Internet gambling operators
require withholding of tax on annual Internet gambling winnings of more than $5,000
Document 2: exempt from minimum wage and maximum hours requirements any employee who has contracted to pay baseball at the minor league level
shall not be construed to require application of maximum hours requirements to any employee who has contracted to play baseball at the major league level
Document 3: establish definitions for 'fighter,' 'combat sport competition,' and 'mixed martial arts'
include individuals who fight in a professional mixed martial arts competition or other professional combat sport competition within the scope of such Act

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Security
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation related to U.S. foreign policy, security assistance, and the promotion of human rights in conflict regions.

Document 1: the Muslim Brotherhood has met the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization
the Department of State should so designate it
Document 2: Prohibits U.S. assistance for Afghanistan unless the United States and Afghanistan enter into a bilateral agreement
Authorizes the President to waive such prohibition if in the U.S. national interest
Document 3: promote the security of Afghan women during the security transition process
increase gender awareness and responsiveness among Afghan National Army (ANA) and Afghan National Police (ANP) personnel

topic.name: Cuba Policy and Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures and policies regarding immigration, broadcasting, and humanitarian aid related to Cuba.

Document 1: Cuban nationals should be treated under the same immigration rules as nationals of other countries
repeals P.L. 89-732, which provides for the adjustment of Cuban citizens or nationals to lawful permanent resident status
Document 2: Prohibits Department of Commerce funds from being used to implement, administer, or enforce specified amendments to Department regulations restricting gift parcels and humanitarian donations to Cuba
the amendments prohibit the sending of gifts of clothing, personal hygiene items, seeds, fishing equipment, soap-making equipment, and veterinary medicine and supplies
Document 3: Repeals the Radio Broadcasting to Cuba Act and the Television Broadcasting to Cuba Act
eliminating Voice of America: Radio/Television Marti broadcasts to Cuba

topic.name: U.S. Citizenship and Health Initiatives
topic.description: Legislation focused on U.S. citizenship verification processes and health-related initiatives for specific populations.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of State to adjudicate U.S. passport applications in an individualized, evidence-based manner.
Prohibits an applicant's race, ethnicity, or ancestry from being taken into account in the passport application adjudicatory process.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to take specified action, directly or through a grant program, with respect to blood disorders in adolescents.
Conduct an education campaign to increase awareness about bleeding disorders among health professionals.
Document 3: Directs the President to report annually to Congress identifying each foreign country that does not use the Stolen and Lost Travel Documents (SLTD) database.
Suspension of assistance for a fiscal year to an identified foreign country.

topic.name: Abandoned Mine Remediation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the cleanup and rehabilitation of abandoned hardrock mines to mitigate environmental pollution.

Document 1: Good Samaritan Cleanup of Abandoned Hardrock Mines Act of 2007
authorize the permitting authority... to issue a Good Samaritan discharge permit
Document 2: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977
repeal the $15 million limitation placed upon the total annual payment
Document 3: Jackson Gulch Rehabilitation Act of 2008
to pay the federal share of the cost of carrying out the Jackson Gulch rehabilitation project

topic.name: Toxic Exposure and Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at addressing health risks and compensation related to toxic substance exposure for workers and military personnel.

Document 1: Advisory Board on Toxic Substances and Worker Health
compensation for lung disease resulting from exposure to toxic substances.
Document 2: open pit burn registry for members of the Armed Forces
health effects of such exposure
Document 3: reduce the average concentration of respirable dust
prevent new incidences and further development of respiratory disease

topic.name: Volunteer Healthcare Liability Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing liability protections for volunteer healthcare professionals serving in underserved areas or during disasters.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to deem volunteer practitioners...
extends the liability protections of the Federal Tort Claims Act to such practitioners.
Document 2: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to shield a health care professional from liability...
the professional is serving as a volunteer in response to a disaster.

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Public Services
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at improving public services and judicial processes within various sectors such as water management, bankruptcy courts, and child welfare.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to extend a contract for water services
contract for water services between the United States and the East Bench Irrigation District
Document 2: amends the federal judicial code to convert certain temporary bankruptcy judges to permanent bankruptcy judges
authorize the appointment of additional bankruptcy judges in Delaware and Michigan
Document 3: Amends the Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention Act of 1974
to promote the well-being of maltreated infants and toddlers and their families

topic.name: Healthcare and Education Legislation
topic.description: This cluster includes legislation focused on improving healthcare access and educational resources for underserved populations.

Document 1: Charity Care for the Uninsured Act of 2007 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow physicians a tax credit for providing charity medical care.
Provides for a credit amount of between $1,000 and $2,000, depending upon the hours of charity care provided.
Document 2: Permanent Marriage Penalty Relief Act of 2007 - Makes provisions of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 that eliminate the marriage penalty in the standard deduction, the 15-percent tax bracket, and the earned income tax credit, permanent.
Document 3: Prescribe A Book Act - Creates a Pediatric Early Literacy program under which the Secretary of Education awards matching grants to nonprofit organizations for the implementation of three-part models.
Health care providers encourage parents to read aloud to their children and offer them recommendations and strategies for doing so.

topic.name: Veteran and Refugee Assistance Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative efforts aimed at providing immigration benefits and support to refugees and veterans and their families.

Document 1: Makes 3,000 immigrant visas available for individuals who were born in Tibet.
Considers individuals born in Tibet or their descendants as natives.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exempt from limitations the sons and daughters of Filipino World War II veterans.

topic.name: Coastal and Marine Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the management and conservation of coastal and marine resources, addressing both ecological and economic aspects.

Document 1: extends the final planting date established by the Risk Management Agency for certain federal crop insurance policies covering grain sorghum
to account for extreme weather conditions
Document 2: renames the Public Lands Corps as the Public Lands Service Corps
permits Corps participants to work in the National Marine Sanctuary System, coral reefs, and other coastal, estuarine, and marine habitats
Document 3: directs the President to establish a National Integrated Coastal and Ocean Observation System
improve the ability to measure, track, explain, and predict weather and climate change and natural climate variability

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Public Welfare
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing public welfare through civil rights recognition, tax administration integrity, and water efficiency improvements.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct a special resource study of the home of the late civil rights activist Medgar Evers.
Evaluate the national significance of such site and determine the suitability and feasibility of designating it as a unit of the National Park System.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to prohibit the IRS from hiring any contractors or designating other non-IRS employees to receive summoned books, papers, records, or other data.
Protects the integrity of tax administration.
Document 3: Requires the Department of Energy to expand its Industrial Assessment Centers program to provide assessment services to water and wastewater treatment facilities.
Identifies opportunities for potential savings for those sites from energy efficiency improvements.

topic.name: Rural Development and Conservation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing rural areas through business support, farmland preservation, and transportation planning.

Document 1: HUBZone Expansion Act of 2013 - Amends the Small Business Act to include as a base closure area for purposes of the HUBZONE program.
historically underutilized business zone program of the Small Business Administration
Document 2: Amends the Farm Security Act of 1985 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to reserve at least 15% of farmland protection program funds for grants.
support cooperative efforts by an eligible state agency, county, or other eligible entity to purchase conservation easements
Document 3: Requires states, at a minimum, to cooperate with affected nonmetropolitan local officials responsible for transportation.
develop and implement long-range statewide transportation plans and statewide transportation improvement programs

topic.name: Support for Local Business Development
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing local business opportunities and access to financing for agricultural and small business initiatives.

Document 1: establish a local farm business and market garden loan program
assist producers in establishing local farm business projects
Document 2: exclude from the meaning of 'member business loan' certain small business loans
increase their participation in the SBA small business loan program
Document 3: create a net worth certificate program that will settle the financial markets
certify that a bank eligible for the program is both in danger of failing

topic.name: Veteran and Public Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing veteran healthcare services and ensuring public safety through justice measures.

Document 1: better health care can be provided to veterans
revising the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) fee-based system
Document 2: offer a reward to any individual who furnishes information
prohibits such Department from offering a reward under this Act
Document 3: establishes in the Department of the Treasury the Office of Public Finance
requires the Director of such Office to carry out a program to provide reinsurance for insured losses

topic.name: Military and Veterans Affairs
topic.description: Legislation focused on military conduct, veterans' outreach, and family reunification efforts related to military conflicts.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense to review material that is not deemed to be sexually explicit
Prohibits print periodicals that regularly feature or give prominence to nudity or sexual or excretory activities.
Document 2: Directs the Department of State to consult with South Korean officials on potential opportunities to reunite Korean American families
Consult with representatives of these Korean American families regarding reunification efforts.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Veterans to establish a separate account for the funding of outreach activities
Authorize the Secretary to award grants to appropriate state veterans agencies for such purpose.

topic.name: National Security and Advisory Committees
topic.description: Documents focus on the establishment and functions of advisory committees related to national security and telecommunications.

Document 1: establish the National Telecommunications and Information Security Advisory Committee
information and advice on national security telecommunications
Document 3: establishes the United States Commission on Planetary Defense
review the structure, coordination, management policies, and procedures of the federal government

topic.name: Environmental Regulation of Energy Production
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the environmental regulations governing energy production methods such as carbon dioxide sequestration and hydraulic fracturing.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise requirements for the secure geological storage of carbon dioxide
the IRS regulations must consider the carbon dioxide to be disposed of in secure geological storage
Document 2: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to repeal the exemption from restrictions on underground injection
Requires state underground injection programs to direct a person conducting hydraulic fracturing operations to disclose to the state the chemicals intended for use
Document 3: Repeals the prohibition on the use of funds to prepare or publish final regulations regarding a commercial leasing program for oil shale
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue all regulations necessary to implement the Oil Share, Tar Sands, and Other Strategic Unconventional Fuels Act of 2005

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Public Benefit
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at promoting public welfare through environmental restoration, artistic contributions, and health initiatives.

Document 1: Wyoming Forest and Watershed Restoration Act of 2007 authorizes restoration services.
Treatment of insect-infected trees and reduction of hazardous fuels.
Document 2: Artist-Museum Partnership Act allows tax deductions for artistic contributions.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code for tax-exempt organizations.
Document 3: SUNSET Act of 2008 allows voluntary HIV testing for veterans.
Repeals prohibitions on widespread HIV testing.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing unfair or deceptive practices in consumer transactions.

Document 3: Declares it unlawful for a supplier to sell crude oil, gasoline, or petroleum distillates at an unconscionably excessive price.
States it is unlawful for any person to employ, in connection with the wholesale purchase or sale of crude oil, gasoline, or petroleum distillates, any manipulative or deceptive device.

topic.name: Student Athlete Health and Safety
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving health and safety protocols for student athletes, particularly concerning concussions and related injuries.

Document 1: require state Medicaid plans to require staff working with individuals with a developmental disability or traumatic brain injury to call emergency services in the event of a life-threatening situation.
Document 2: requires annual baseline concussion testing of student athletes on the active roster of teams participating in contact/collision or limited-contact/impact sports before they participate in any contact drills or activities.

requires athletically-related student aid provided to student athletes who play contact/collision sports to be guaranteed for the duration of their attendance at the school, up to four years, and irrevocable for reasons related to skill or injury.
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to states for establishing, disseminating, and ensuring the implementation by schools of concussion management guidelines
funding implementation by schools of computerized preseason baseline and post-injury neuropsychological testing for student athletes.

topic.name: Regulation of Hazardous Materials
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating hazardous substances and their impact on public health and safety.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug unless the applicant demonstrates that there is a reasonably certainty of no harm to human health due to the development of antimicrobial resistance.
Defines 'critical antimicrobial animal drug' as a drug intended for use in food-producing animals that contains specified antibiotics.
Document 2: Establishes within the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) an Office of Flood Research and Policy, headed by a Director.
Make recommendations and identify best practices for predicting, preventing, and minimizing flooding and its effects.
Document 3: Amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary of Homeland Security to regulate the sale and transfer of ammonium nitrate.
Requires the Secretary to establish a threshold percentage for ammonium nitrate in a substance.

topic.name: Regulatory Acts for Safety and Innovation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative measures aimed at ensuring safety in consumer products, enhancing healthcare reporting, and promoting innovation in the textile industry.

Document 1: Protect Children from Dangerous Lighters Act of 2009 - Treats certain novelty lighters as banned hazardous substances under the Federal Hazardous Substances Act.

Defines as a 'novelty lighter' a lighter that has a toy-like appearance, has entertaining audio or visual effects, or resembles in any way in form or function an item that is commonly recognized as appealing, attractive, or intended for use by children of 10 years of age or younger.
Document 2: Flexibility in Electronic Health Record Reporting Act - Requires the Department of Health and Human Services, with respect to electronic health record (EHR) meaningful use incentive programs under Medicare and Medicaid, to continue through 2016 to allow the use of quarterly reporting without regard to the payment year or the stage of meaningful use criteria involved.
Document 3: American Textile Technology Innovation and Research for Exportation (ATTIRE) Act - Directs the Secretary of Commerce to establish a grant program to fund textile research and innovation in the U.S. textile and fiber products industry.
Gives preference in the award of such grants to collaborative research organizations that emphasize peer-reviewed research by leading academic and industry experts.

topic.name: Federal Program Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing transparency, reporting, and accountability of federal programs and their performance.

Document 1: Revises requirements for providing information about federal programs to increase taxpayer understanding of the cost and performance of such programs.

Requires the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to include on its website a program inventory that identifies each program of the federal government for which there is more than $1 million in annual budget authority.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of State to report quarterly to Congress describing efforts by Mexico to meet the treaty obligations of Mexico to deliver water to the Rio Grande.
Report annually to Congress describing the benefits to the United States of the 2012 document entitled 'Interim International Cooperative Measures in the Colorado River Basin through 2017.'
Document 3: Amends the Coastal Zone Management Act of 1972 to establish a coastal climate change adaptation planning and response program.
Provides financial and technical assistance and training to enable coastal states to implement those plans.

topic.name: Workforce and Education Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing workforce development, education, and safety standards in various sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to extend the Constitution, laws, and civil and political jurisdiction of the United States to installations attached to the seabed of the outer Continental Shelf (OCS)
energy leases on the OCS shall be maintained and issued only under the OCSLA.
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to establish a geography education grant program
designed to expand geographic literacy among kindergarten through grade 12 students by improving their teachers' professional development programs.
Document 3: Directs the Attorney General to establish a method to permit employers in the electronic life safety and security systems installation and monitoring industry to request state and federal fingerprint-based background checks
Requires that such method allow employers to discover if employees or prospective employees have been convicted of a felony or of an offense involving dishonesty.

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Social Issues
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing significant social issues such as health care, education, and military policies in the United States.

Document 1: Breast Cancer Research Stamp Authorization Act - Extends provisions requiring the U.S. Postal Service to issue a special postage stamp to contribute funding for breast cancer research.
special postage stamp for first-class mail that costs not less than 15% more than the regular first-class stamp
Document 2: Don't Ask, Don't Tell Repeal Act of 2010 - Provides for repeal of the current Department of Defense (DOD) policy concerning homosexuality in the Armed Forces.
the President, Secretary, and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff (JCS) certify to the congressional defense committees that they have considered the report and proposed plan of action
Document 3: After School for America's Children Act - Amends the 21st Century Community Learning Centers program to provide students with before and afterschool programs to improve their academic performance.
Requires the Centers to provide students with activities that are targeted to their academic needs and aligned with the instruction they receive during the school day.

topic.name: Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting marine and freshwater environments from industrial activities and ensuring sustainable management of natural resources.

Document 1: Prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for the exploration, development, or production of oil, natural gas, or any other mineral
Clean Ocean and Safe Tourism Anti-Drilling Act
Document 2: Designates the U.S. exclusive economic zone
Ronald Wilson Reagan Exclusive Economic Zone of the United States
Document 3: Directs the Chief of the Army Corps of Engineers to revise the Missouri River Mainstem Reservoir System Master Water Control Manual
Ensure that the System's flood control storage allocation has been recalculated

topic.name: Legal Protections and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring legal accountability and protections for individuals involved in judicial processes, particularly concerning victims, witnesses, and the integrity of legal proceedings.

Document 1: Prohibits a court from dismissing an action against a manufacturer, seller, or trade association for damages or relief resulting from an alleged defect or negligence with respect to a product.
Makes the contents of the Firearms Trace System database subject to subpoena or other discovery and admissible as evidence.
Document 2: Requires the head of a federal agency to approve in writing any conference costing more than $25,000.
Publish a summary on the agency website of the conference that includes the purpose and total cost of the conference.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to impose criminal penalties on any person who kills, or attempts to kill, a witness in a state or local judicial proceeding.
Directs the U.S. Sentencing Commission to amend guidelines to increase the sentencing range for obstruction of justice if such crime involved threatening, harming, or bribing a witness.

topic.name: U.S. Legislation on Palestine and Israel
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative measures regarding financial aid and diplomatic relations with the Palestinian Authority and the Palestine Liberation Organization in the context of their interactions with Israel.

Document 1: prohibits any amounts from being obligated or expended to provide assistance to the Palestinian Authority
until the PA withdraws its request to join the International Criminal Court.
Document 2: expresses the sense of Congress concerning the participation of the Palestine Liberation Organization and the Palestinian Authority in the peace process
the PLO and the PA no longer provide financial awards or salaries to Palestinians imprisoned in Israel for terrorist attacks.

topic.name: Public Health Initiatives
